text: Parkour was developed in France in the 1980s by Raymond Belle and later by his son David Belle and his friends. It is

explanation: عبارتی برای نشان دادن منبع یا شالوده یک ایده یا سیستم.
یک سری از موانع فیزیکی که فرد یا تیم باید از آن‌ها عبور کند، معمولاً در تمرینات نظامی.

text: training. The aim is to get from one point to another in any way you can, and

explanation: نشان دادن شخصیت یا احساسات از طریق فعالیت‌های خلاقانه یا فیزیکی.

text: while doing so. There are no limits on how you move in parkour: running, climbing, swinging, jumping, rolling and any other types of movement are possible. Parkour is more of a

explanation: مجموعه‌ای از عقاید یا دیدگاه‌ها درباره چگونگی زندگی یا انجام یک فعالیت.

text: or set of ideas than a sport. It is a new way of seeing the environment and finding ways to go over, under, around, across or through obstacles.
Parkour can be done alone or in groups. The most famous group of

explanation: واژه‌ای فرانسوی که به افرادی که ورزش پارکور را انجام می‌دهند اطلاق می‌شود.

text: (people who do parkour) were the Yamakasi, a group

explanation: زمانی استفاده می‌شود که بخواهیم اجزای تشکیل‌دهنده یک گروه را معرفی کنیم.

text: David Belle and his friends and cousins. They formed in the late 1980s and became popular during the 1990s and 2000s after appearing in several films, documentaries and advertisements. In the Yamakasi, there were

explanation: قوانینی که باید دقیقاً رعایت شوند و تخطی از آن‌ها جایز نیست.

text: . Members had to arrive on time and they were not allowed to complain or

explanation: تراشیدن دلایل غیرواقعی یا ضعیف برای توجیه یک خطا یا کوتاهی.
کیفیت فروتن بودن و نداشتن غرور بیش از حد؛ یک ارزش اخلاقی مهم در پارکور.

text: , so they were not allowed to

explanation: تلاش برای جلب توجه یا تحسین دیگران با نمایش توانایی‌ها یا دارایی‌ها.
یک نگرش ذهنی یا وضعیت روانی در یک زمان خاص.

text: . It is about getting over mental as well as physical

explanation: هر چیزی که مانع حرکت یا پیشرفت شود، چه به صورت فیزیکی و چه ذهنی.

text: . It teaches people to touch the world and

explanation: ارتباط برقرار کردن یا اثر متقابل گذاشتن بر چیزی یا کسی.

text: it. It is about understanding what it means to be human. The organisation Parkour.net believes that parkour can never be a competitive sport. It is an art and is

explanation: وقتی چیزی بر موضوع یا هدف خاصی تمرکز دارد.

text: self-development. They say you can't ask, 'Who is the best at parkour?' Raymond Belle's advice is: 'If two roads open up before you, always take the more difficult one. Because you know you can travel the easy one.'
There are some gyms and camps where you can practise and learn parkour. However, many traceurs do not like the idea of special places for their activity. The idea behind parkour is to

translation: وفق یافتن با / سازگار شدن با
explanation: تغییر دادن رفتار یا روش خود برای موفقیت در یک موقعیت یا محیط جدید.

text: any environment and be creative about how you get through it. It is about freedom and self-expression. The founder of parkour refused to teach people how to do moves or get over obstacles. The whole point is to learn your own technique and way of moving. So the idea of having classes or a limited space to practise in

explanation: زمانی که دو ایده، عقیده یا وضعیت با هم همخوانی ندارند و متناقض هستند.

text: Parkour is also known as freerunning. Sometimes freerunning

explanation: برای توصیف یا نامیدن چیزی به کار می‌رود.

text: another form of parkour developed by Sebastien Foucan, which has more focus on the individual. The term freerunning

explanation: نشان‌دهنده منشأ یا مبدأ پیدایش یک اصطلاح یا ایده.

text: the film Jump London (2003). It told the story of three French traceurs practising parkour around the famous monuments of London. Freerunning was the English translation of parkour. There are more similarities than differences between the two activities, and the Parkour UK website uses the two terms to refer to the same activity.
